description_long: This radio receiver features a rectangular metal chassis with a brushed silver front panel, accented by wooden side panels that add a touch of vintage style. The front face is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a large tuning dial, all arranged symmetrically for user interaction. A blue-lit frequency display window is present on the left side of the front panel, providing clear visual feedback for tuning. The top surface has several ventilation slots, likely for heat dissipation, and the overall build suggests a robust, high-quality construction. The rear and side panels are plain, with the wood grain visible on the sides. This object is reminiscent of classic hi-fi stereo receivers commonly found in home audio setups or retro-themed urban interiors.
description: A vintage-style radio receiver with a silver front panel, wooden side panels, multiple knobs, and a blue-lit frequency display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the silver panel, blue-lit display, multiple knobs, and wooden side accents.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wooden panel and top ventilation slots, with minimal front detail visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view showing the full extent of the top ventilation slots and the rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with wooden panel and top slots visible, and some front controls partially seen.
